name: Denmark
description: Denmark is a global leader in design, sustainability, and innovation. Its universities consistently rank among Europe's best, and the country boasts a high quality of life with a strong focus on work-life balance.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Living costs will add up to around 700 - 900 EUR monthly in smaller towns. In larger areas, like Copenhagen, living costs could reach up to 1200 EUR a month.
tuitionFees.postgraduate: Entry Requirements, Duration of the programs and Tuition Fee
tuitionFees.phd: Fully funded as a 3-year PhD employee at Danish universities

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Denmark?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Denmark is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Denmark?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
